channel.title: 1950s on Alien Truth Social
channel.description: Recent content in 1950s on Alien Truth Social

item.title: Otis T. Carr and His Flying Saucer Claims
item.description:
Otis T. Carr emerged in the 1950s flying saucer scene, founding OTC Enterprises in 1955 with the stated aim of advancing technology inspired by Nikola Tesla. Carr patented a flying saucer design, the Ezechiel Wheel, and claimed to be developing a full-size version capable of lunar travel. He also invented other devices like “The Gravity Electric Generator” and “The Utron Electric battery.” Despite receiving significant publicity, including from Fate Magazine, Carr’s business ventures were considered fraudulent. He was convicted in 1961 for selling unregistered securities and served part of a jail term.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Otis_T._Carr

item.title: The Seekers: A UFO Religion in Mid-Twentieth Century United States
item.description:
The Seekers, also known as The Brotherhood of the Seven Rays, was a UFO religion founded in 1953 by Charles Laughead in the Midwestern United States. Led by Dorothy Martin, the group believed flying saucers would save them from a predicted catastrophe on December 21, 1954. When the prophecy failed, many members experienced cognitive dissonance, a phenomenon studied by Leon Festinger in his book When Prophecy Fails. The Seekers are considered by some to be the earliest UFO religion.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Seekers_%28rapturists%29

item.title: UFO: The True Story of Flying Saucers
item.description:
UFO (full title: Unidentified Flying Objects: The True Story of Flying Saucers) is a 1956 American semi-documentary film exploring the UFO phenomenon in the United States. The film begins by recounting early publicized UFO sightings and the US Air Force’s initial investigations, including Project Sign. It then follows Albert M. Chop, a public information officer, as his views on UFOs evolve from skepticism to belief in their unknown, possibly extraterrestrial, origin. The documentary analyzes notable UFO footage and culminates with a recreation of the 1952 Washington, D.C. UFO incident, in which Chop played a significant role.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UFO_%281956_film%29

item.title: Civilian Saucer Intelligence
item.description:
Civilian Saucer Intelligence (CSI) was an independent UFO research group founded in New York City in 1954. Unlike many amateur groups, CSI conducted rigorous investigations and published a well-regarded quarterly newsletter. The organization was critical of contactees but distinct in its investigation of close encounters involving alleged animate beings. CSI’s core personnel included Ted Bloecher, Isabel Davis, and Alexander Mebane, and the group was instrumental in translating French ufologist Aimé Michel’s books into English. CSI was defunct by 1959, though Davis and Bloecher remained active in UFO research.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Civilian_Saucer_Intelligence

item.title: Wonsan-Sunchon UFO Incident
item.description:
On the night of January 29, 1952, during the Korean War, four American military personnel reported seeing an orange globe-shaped light over Wonsan and Sunchon. Air Force UFO experts believe this incident contributed to the 1952 UFO flap. The sightings occurred amid fears of new Soviet technology and during a stalemate in the war, with Wonsan under naval blockade and Sunchon experiencing a major air battle. The incident gained nationwide attention through United Press and was discussed in publications like Time and Life magazines, with the Air Force confirming an ongoing investigation.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wonsan-Sunchon_UFO_incident
